pattern: A hash map of already-seen values turns the pair search into O(1) complement lookups, trading O(n) space for O(n) time.
hints: For each value you visit, there is exactly one other value that would complete the pair. What is it?
You keep asking "have I already seen target minus the current value?". Which structure answers membership in constant time?
Scan once, keeping a dict of value -> index. Before recording nums[i], check whether target - nums[i] is already a key; if so you have both indices.

edge_inputs.description: two equal values are the pair
answer is not at index 0, and an element may not be reused
repeated zero must use two distinct indices

stress.description: 10k elements with the unique pair planted at the end - exposes O(n^2) scans
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    a = rng.randrange(-10**8, 10**8)
    b = a + 1
    target = a + b
    forbidden = {target - x for x in (a, b)}
    nums = []
    while len(nums) < n - 2:
        v = rng.randrange(-10**9, 10**9)
        if v in (a, b) or v in forbidden:
            continue
        nums.append(v)
        forbidden.add(target - v)
    i = rng.randrange(0, len(nums) + 1)
    nums.insert(i, a)
    j = rng.randrange(0, len(nums) + 1)
    nums.insert(j, b)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: exactly one valid pair by construction; should finish well under the run timeout
